source_pr.title: *: Support List default partition | tidb-test=pr/2110
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #20679

Problem Summary:
Currently all values must be defined in the partitioning definitions, which is not very user friendly. By allowing a DEFAULT partition, as a catch-all partition, one only need to define the most occurring values and have the DEFAULT partition catch the rest.

### What is changed and how it works?
For insert/update, the pruning is simple, if no match on defined values and there exists a default partition, put the new value there.

For select pruning, we will add the default partition if the existing values are not matched or there are multiple partitioning columns (due to the combination check needs a further refactoring) or if there is a range.

Since this is a compatibility breaker for MySQL (but not completely for MariaDB), I also added `tidb_enable_default_list_partition` variable that is default OFF.

One difference from the syntax of MariaDB and Oracle is that it allows both `(DEFAULT)` and `(<list of values>)` in a partition definition, which if used, it is not supported by MariaDB (I'm OK to remove this enhancement, since functionally it does not make sense, but it may be better usability to have the known values there, instead of like in a comment).

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [x] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [x] Changes MySQL compatibility

#### Manual testing
```bash
make
tiup playground nightly --db.binpath ~/repos/tidb/bin/tidb-server
```
```mysql
drop table if exists t;
set tidb_enable_default_list_partition=1;
create table t
(a bigint unsigned,
 b bigint unsigned,
 c varchar(768),
 primary key (a,b),
 key (c))
partition by list(b)
(partition p1 values in (0,1,4,5,6,7,9),
 partition p2 values in (11,13,12,15,16,17,18),
 partition p3 values in (22,21,default,66,220));
insert into t values (1,1,"Z"),(2,2,"y"),(3,3,"q"),(4,4,"R"),(5,5,"M");
insert into t values (15,15,"W"),(17,17,"N"),(220,220,"T"),(66,66,"o"),(10,10,"z");
insert into t values (6,6,"p"),(7,7,"B"),(8,8,"a"),(9,9,"i"),(11,11,"n");
analyze table t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,0),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t; 
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,1),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,2),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,3),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,4),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,5),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,6),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,7),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,8),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,9),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,10),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,11),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,12),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,13),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,14),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,15),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,16),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,17),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,18),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t order by a,b limit 1600000;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,19),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t order by a,b limit 1600000;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,20),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t order by a,b limit 1600000;
insert into t select `a`+pow(2,21),b,concat(`a`,`c`) from t order by a,b limit 1600000;
select count(*), sum(a), sum(b) from t;
# +----------+---------------+----------+
# | count(*) | sum(a)        | sum(b)   |
# +----------+---------------+----------+
# | 10332160 | 7148444658184 | 264327144 |
# +----------+---------------+----------+
```
```bash
 ./bin/br restore full --storage 'local:///backup/defaultlist'
```
Then drop the table, or start a new cluster
```mysql
drop table t;
```
And Restore
```bash
./bin/br restore full --storage 'local:///backup/defaultlist'
```
And check the table contains the same
```mysql
select count(*), sum(a), sum(b) from t;
# +----------+---------------+----------+
# | count(*) | sum(a)        | sum(b)   |
# +----------+---------------+----------+
# | 10332160 | 7148444658184 | 264327144 |
# +----------+---------------+----------+
```
### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Adding support for a default partition in LIST [COLUMNS] partitioning.
```

symptoms: EXPLAIN/statistics-related golden output could differ under different RPC encoding settings, making the planner test unstable (planner/core/partition_pruner_test.go).
failure_signature: Statistics EXPLAIN output differs depending on RPC encoding (planner/core/partition_pruner_test.go).
root_cause_explanation: The planner test is sensitive to exact EXPLAIN/statistics output. The patch notes that default RPC encoding can make statistics EXPLAIN results differ, causing the golden-plan test to be unstable. The stabilization forces a deterministic configuration by explicitly setting `tidb_enable_chunk_rpc = on` before running the EXPLAIN assertions, reducing environment/config-dependent variation (planner/core/partition_pruner_test.go).
fix_pattern: Force a deterministic RPC encoding/config (`tidb_enable_chunk_rpc = on`) before asserting EXPLAIN/statistics output in the planner golden tests.

evidence: evidence: + // Default RPC encoding may cause statistics explain result differ and then the test unstable. | sort.Slice(infos, func(i, j int) bool { (planner/core/partition_pruner_test.go)
